Xi Jinping:
Biography of Xi Jinping
Birth and Childhood:
Xi Jinping (Born: 18 August, 1953) was born in Beijing, China. His father Xi Jinhuan was a high-ranking official of the Communist Party of China and his mother Xi Liang was a teacher. His childhood and youth were spent amid China’s cultural crisis and communal divisions.
Education and Early Career:
In 1969, he joined agricultural work in Shaanxi province of China, where he participated in agricultural work and social work. In 1975, he received education in chemical technology at Tsinghua University and obtained a degree in 1975. Later, he worked in a coal materials factory in Shaanxi province.
Early Political Career:
In 1974, he became a member of the Communist Party of China. In 1979, he worked in China’s military and later took up political work in various provinces of China. In the 1980s, he worked in Shandong province and later became the chief minister of Shandong province.
Political Advancement:
In the 1980s, he worked in various provinces of China, where he developed economic understanding in the fields of social and economic development. In the 1990s, he became the chief minister of Shandong province and later became the chief minister of Shanghai city of China.
